\mbox{}\rm\mc {\tt <}{\tt !}{\tt -}{\tt -}\  \parbox[t]{0.85\textwidth}{\ \centerline{\bf Towers of Hanoi --- HTML{\ /}CSS{\ /}JavaScript}} \rm\mc \ {\tt -}{\tt -}{\tt >}

\tt\mc 

\noindent
\mbox{}\rm\mc \ \begin{quote}
\noindent
This is a fully working implementation of the Towers of Hanoi
puzzle that runs in any modern web browser.  The recursive algorithm
moves $n$ discs from pole A to pole C via pole B, requiring exactly
$2^n - 1$ moves.  Open this file in a browser to see it in action.
\end{quote} 
\tt\mc 

\noindent
\mbox{}\rm\mc \ {\tt -}{\tt -}{\tt >}
